original_abstract: This review explores recent advancements in the synthesis of chiral nanographenes, with a particular focus on emerging literature and less-frequently discussed methodologies. While the Scholl reaction remains a cornerstone of polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbon synthesis, this survey prioritizes alternative, non-Scholl strategies that offer unique control over molecular curvature and axial chirality. By highlighting these unconventional synthetic pathways, we provide a critical overview of current trends and future directions in the preparation of well-defined, enantiopure nanographene architectures.
humanized_abstract: Recent developments in the synthesis of chiral nanographenes have been reviewed with a strong emphasis on new literature and less common methods. Although the Scholl reaction is still the prevalent method to synthesize polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons, non-Scholl based approaches are considered, here, in order to better control the curvature of the molecule and hence the axial chirality. Less common methods for synthesizing well-defined, enantiopure nanostructures are thus highlighted and discussed within the context of the current developments in the field.
